probe_alternatives.tactic: move=> &2 r1 _ eL _; split=> [rR _|_ z1L _]; [by ring | split; [by ring | move=> _; smt(expD expM)]].
probe_alternatives.how_to_use: This tactic was rejected by a read-only probe on the current proof state. Repair it before probing again.

obligation_families.effect: The current proof point is dominated by local logical obligations rather than program-frontier movement.
obligation_families.limitations: Quantifier names are not normalized across EasyCrypt pretty-printer variants.

translation_facts: Decorations identify the program memory from which a term is read.
Pretty-printer memory names and introduced proof-context names can differ.
ambient_memory_translation.effect: Local claims in the pure tail depend on the currently introduced memory binders as well as displayed term decorations.

pure_tail_surface.effect: Summarizes verifier-visible pure-goal structure after program frontier actions have been discharged or hidden by local proof steps.
limitations: This panel records syntactic and proof-state facts from the current view.
It does not choose a tactic or instantiate a lemma.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: proof-state analysis sees no live call sites after a prior `inline *` at tactic 3. Continue at procedure/ambient layer, or undo if a call lemma is needed.

evidence: pure_tail_surface is visible for the current goal
current proof state has no program-frontier work before the logical residual

related_checkpoints.label: After seq opened / before branch work #13
related_checkpoints.why_checkpoint: seq-local branch boundary; selecting it keeps the seq cut and removes branch-local work after it

related_checkpoints.label: Before seq cut #12
related_checkpoints.why_checkpoint: seq-cut boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this cut was committed

limitations: does not select a destructor, witness, rewrite, or SMT lemma
reported checkpoints are recovery references, not a default route

proof_piece_memory.interpretation: This is manager-owned evidence about prior route repairs and daemon-verified route options. Replayable pieces should still be verifier-checked before commit; stale and negative pieces are warnings, not tactics.

lineage_briefing.interpretation: Manager-owned continuity notes for this node. Use them as resume/navigation evidence; EasyCrypt replay still decides whether any old route piece is valid.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: The latest transition recorded an error.
ask_manager_for.returns: manager-provided read-only context for this proof route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
Need local or standard-library lemma names before rewriting a pure goal.
ask_manager_for.returns: nearby lemma hints and qualified-name lookup guidance

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid EasyCrypt form for rewrite, apply, conseq, while, or SMT setup.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

items.label: Restore before last checkpoint rewind
items.why_checkpoint: state saved before the last checkpoint rewind; selecting it returns to that proof state
items.effect_if_selected: This restores the branch to the proof state saved before the last checkpoint rewind.

items.label: Before latest call-obligation tactic #22
items.why_checkpoint: call-obligation local boundary; selecting it keeps the opened call obligation and removes later local work
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #22 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: After seq opened / before branch work #13
items.why_checkpoint: seq-local branch boundary; selecting it keeps the seq cut and removes branch-local work after it
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #13 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: After call opened / before obligation work #8
items.why_checkpoint: call-local boundary; selecting it keeps the call tactic and removes work committed after the call opened
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #8 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before call invariant #7
items.why_checkpoint: call invariant introduction point; later oracle/branch obligations often reveal missing preserved facts or an over-strong equality from this tactic
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #7 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before seq cut #12
items.why_checkpoint: seq-cut boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this cut was committed
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #12 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before inline expansion #3
items.why_checkpoint: inline boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this expansion
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #3 and every committed tactic after it in this node.
